summary: sms search --from and --to are now intersected when both are supplied, instead of silently dropping --from.
reason: The previous identifier resolution was `identifier := to; if identifier == "" { identifier = from }`, which meant that `sms search "X" --from +A --to +B` silently discarded --from and only restricted to conversations involving +B. Callers expecting the natural intersection (conversations involving BOTH numbers) got a quietly broader result set. Fix: a three-case switch -- both flags -> intersect the two participant sets; single flag -> filter on that one; no flag -> no participant restriction. Direction-aware filtering (--from for outgoing, --to for incoming) is left as a follow-up because the synced message rows don't expose a uniform direction field. Surfaced by Greptile P1 in the PR #417 fifth review pass.
validated_outcome: go build ./... succeeds; go test ./... passes; go vet clean.
